tasks.title: Upgrade WebContainer API to v1.6.1
tasks.description: Update the @webcontainers/api package from the current version to v1.6.1 to leverage the latest features and performance improvements.
tasks.details:
1. Check the current version of @webcontainers/api in package.json
2. Update the dependency in package.json: `"@webcontainers/api": "^1.6.1"`
3. Run package manager update command (npm install or yarn)
4. Review the codebase for any breaking changes that might be introduced by the upgrade
5. Pay special attention to file system operations and any deprecated methods
6. Update imports if necessary
7. Test basic WebContainer functionality after the upgrade
tasks.testStrategy:
1. Verify the package is correctly updated in node_modules
2. Create a simple test case that initializes a WebContainer instance
3. Test basic file system operations to ensure compatibility
4. Run existing tests that involve WebContainer functionality
5. Monitor for any console errors related to the WebContainer API

subtasks.title: Preparation Phase
subtasks.description: Analyze current WebContainer API implementation and prepare for upgrade
subtasks.details: Verify current version of @webcontainer/api package in use. Review API documentation for changes between versions. Check for any deprecation notices or breaking changes. Document current functionality that relies on WebContainer API. Create a backup of the current implementation.

subtasks.title: Implementation Phase
subtasks.description: Update the WebContainer API package and implement necessary changes
subtasks.details: Update @webcontainer/api package to the latest version using npm or yarn. Modify code to accommodate any breaking changes or new features. Update any dependencies that might be affected by the WebContainer API upgrade. Ensure proper error handling for any new or changed API behaviors.

subtasks.title: Testing Phase
subtasks.description: Verify functionality and compatibility after the upgrade
subtasks.details:
Run unit tests to verify basic functionality. Perform integration testing to ensure compatibility with other components. Test edge cases and error handling. Verify performance metrics to ensure no degradation. Document any changes in behavior or new features available after the upgrade.
<info added on 2025-05-10T17:04:03.348Z>
Run unit tests to verify basic functionality. Perform integration testing to ensure compatibility with other components. Test edge cases and error handling. Verify performance metrics to ensure no degradation. Document any changes in behavior or new features available after the upgrade.

Test plan based on changelog review:
1. Verify WebContainer instance initialization post-upgrade
2. Test core `fs` operations:
   - `readFile` functionality
   - `writeFile` functionality on test files
   - Note any issues that might require full `mount` refactor
3. Specifically test `fs.watch` functionality:
   - Verify `watchPaths` in `files.ts` works as expected
   - Document any behavioral changes or errors for Task 5
4. Test critical WebContainer interactions:
   - Process spawning via `webcontainerInstance.spawn`
   - Port event listening via `webcontainerInstance.on('port')`
   - Reference existing patterns in `ActionRunner` and `PreviewsStore`
5. Create comprehensive documentation of all findings, including:
   - Any behavioral changes
   - New features or capabilities
   - Potential regressions
   - Performance impacts
</info added on 2025-05-10T17:04:03.348Z>

tasks.title: Implement File System Mounting for Initial Templates
tasks.description: Utilize the webcontainerInstance.mount() method to efficiently load initial template files into the WebContainer at page load.
tasks.testStrategy:
1. Measure and compare file loading time before and after implementation
2. Verify all expected files are correctly mounted in the WebContainer
3. Test with various template sizes to ensure consistent performance
4. Check for any errors during the mounting process
5. Validate that mounted files are accessible and have correct content

subtasks.title: Template Preparation and Analysis
subtasks.description: Analyze existing templates and prepare them for conversion to FileSystemTree format
subtasks.details: Identify all existing file system templates in the codebase. Document the current structure and mounting approach. Create a mapping document that outlines how each template will be converted to the new FileSystemTree format. Identify any edge cases or special handling required for specific templates.

subtasks.title: Mount Implementation Development
subtasks.description: Develop the core mounting implementation using the new FileSystemTree format
subtasks.details: Create the mounting process implementation following the 5-step process: command execution, validation, inode allocation, connection establishment, and file system table updating. Implement the mount command structure with proper parameter handling for file system type, device name, and mount point. Ensure proper error handling and validation of inputs.

subtasks.title: Performance Optimization
subtasks.description: Optimize the mounting implementation for performance and resource efficiency
subtasks.details: Profile the new implementation to identify performance bottlenecks. Optimize resource allocation during the mounting process. Implement caching mechanisms where appropriate. Ensure efficient handling of concurrent mount requests. Minimize system overhead during the mounting process.

subtasks.title: Testing and Validation
subtasks.description: Develop comprehensive tests and validate the new mounting implementation
subtasks.details: Create unit tests for each component of the mounting process. Develop integration tests to verify proper interaction between components. Test edge cases and error handling. Perform load testing to ensure stability under high demand. Validate that all converted templates function correctly with the new implementation.

tasks.title: Implement Concurrent NPM Package Installation
tasks.description: Modify the loading sequence to initiate NPM package installation concurrently with backend AI code generation, rather than sequentially.
tasks.testStrategy:
1. Measure total load time before and after implementation
2. Verify both processes complete successfully when run concurrently
3. Test error scenarios (e.g., AI generation fails, NPM install fails)
4. Ensure UI correctly reflects the concurrent loading state
5. Test with various package sizes and AI generation complexities

subtasks.title: Current Flow Analysis
subtasks.description: Analyze the existing application loading sequence to identify concurrency bottlenecks and opportunities for parallelization
subtasks.details: Examine the current execution flow, document synchronous operations that can be parallelized, identify potential race conditions in the existing code, and create a flow diagram of the current loading sequence

subtasks.title: Promise-based Concurrency Implementation
subtasks.description: Implement Promise-based concurrency using java.util.concurrent package to optimize application loading
subtasks.details: Utilize ThreadPoolExecutor for managing concurrent tasks, implement CompletableFuture for asynchronous operations, create a task scheduling mechanism using ScheduledThreadPoolExecutor, and develop a strategy for handling task dependencies

subtasks.title: Error Handling and Race Condition Management
subtasks.description: Develop robust error handling mechanisms and strategies to prevent race conditions
subtasks.details: Implement exception handling for asynchronous operations, create timeout mechanisms for long-running tasks, develop synchronization strategies using locks or atomic operations, and implement retry logic for failed operations

subtasks.title: UI State Management Integration
subtasks.description: Integrate the concurrent operations with UI state management to ensure consistent user experience
subtasks.details: Develop a state management system that reflects async operation progress, implement loading indicators for in-progress operations, create a mechanism to update UI components when async operations complete, and ensure UI remains responsive during concurrent operations

tasks.title: Update File Watching Mechanism
tasks.description: Investigate and implement a replacement or alternative for watchPaths compatible with @webcontainers/api v1.6.1.
tasks.testStrategy:
1. Test file change detection with various file types
2. Verify that file changes trigger appropriate reactions in the UI
3. Test performance impact of the watching mechanism
4. Check for memory leaks by creating and destroying watchers
5. Test edge cases like rapid consecutive changes
6. Verify compatibility with other WebContainer operations

subtasks.title: Research File Watching APIs
subtasks.description: Investigate available file watching mechanisms across different platforms and languages
subtasks.details: Compare WatchService API in Java, FileSystemWatcher in .NET, and file-watchers in Racket. Document the core capabilities, event types, and limitations of each approach. Determine which API best suits our requirements based on cross-platform needs, performance considerations, and event granularity.

subtasks.title: Select Implementation Strategy
subtasks.description: Choose between low-level API usage or building a higher-level abstraction
subtasks.details: Evaluate whether to use the WatchService API directly or create a custom abstraction layer. Consider polling-based approaches as fallback if direct file system notifications aren't reliable across all target platforms. Document the tradeoffs between simplicity, performance, and cross-platform compatibility.

subtasks.title: Implement File System Watcher
subtasks.description: Create the core watcher component that monitors directory changes
subtasks.details: Implement the file system watcher using the selected API. Set up the main monitoring loop that registers directories, creates appropriate WatchKey instances, and handles the infinite loop for processing events. Include proper error handling and logging for monitoring failures.

subtasks.title: Develop Event Handler System
subtasks.description: Create handlers for different file system events (create, modify, delete, rename)
subtasks.details: Implement event handlers for each type of file system event. Create a flexible handler registration system that allows custom actions for different event types. Include filtering capabilities to ignore certain files or directories based on patterns.

subtasks.title: Implement Resource Management and Cleanup
subtasks.description: Ensure proper resource handling and graceful shutdown procedures
subtasks.details: Implement proper resource management for the watcher service, including graceful shutdown procedures. Handle thread management for concurrent watchers, ensure WatchService instances are properly closed, and implement cleanup routines for temporary resources. Test shutdown behavior under various scenarios.

tasks.title: Enhance Loading State UI in Chat Component
tasks.description: Update the Chat.client.tsx component to accurately display loading states throughout the entire setup process and disable chat submission during loading.
tasks.details:
1. Review current loading state implementation in Chat.client.tsx
2. Identify all critical loading phases:
   - WebContainer booting
   - Initial file mounting
   - NPM package installation
3. Update the loading state management to track these phases:
```typescript
const [loadingStates, setLoadingStates] = useState({
  webcontainerBooting: true,
  fileMounting: false,
  packageInstallation: false
});

const isLoading = Object.values(loadingStates).some(Boolean);
```
4. Update UI to reflect current loading phase with descriptive messages
5. Disable chat submission interface while isLoading is true:
```jsx
<button 
  type="submit" 
  disabled={isLoading}
  className={isLoading ? 'disabled' : ''}
>
  Submit
</button>
```
6. Add progress indicators for longer operations like package installation
tasks.testStrategy:
1. Test UI rendering for each loading state
2. Verify chat submission is properly disabled during loading
3. Test transitions between loading states
4. Verify loading indicators accurately reflect actual system state
5. Test user experience with various network conditions
6. Ensure loading states are properly reset after errors

subtasks.title: Design State Management for Loading States
subtasks.description: Define and implement a robust state management strategy to track and control loading states across the application. This includes distinguishing between global and component-level loading, handling multiple concurrent loading phases, and ensuring state transitions are clear and predictable.
subtasks.details: Evaluate whether to use a centralized state management library (e.g., Redux, Context API) or local component state. Plan for handling both upfront and user-triggered loading scenarios. Ensure the state model supports progressive loading and can differentiate between indeterminate and determinate loading phases.

subtasks.title: Update UI Components to Reflect Loading States
subtasks.description: Modify existing UI components or create new ones to visually represent loading states based on the state management design. Ensure that loading indicators are scoped appropriately (e.g., page-level, section-level, or inline) and minimize layout shifts.
subtasks.details: Implement skeleton screens, spinners, or placeholders as needed. Place loading indicators near the content they represent and avoid excessive or redundant indicators. Replace loading indicators with actual content or error messages as soon as data is available.

subtasks.title: Implement Progress Indicators and Loading Feedback
subtasks.description: Integrate appropriate progress indicators to communicate loading progress to users. Select indicator types based on expected wait times and user expectations, such as spinners for short waits and progress bars or percentage indicators for longer waits.
subtasks.details: For short waits (1–3 seconds), use indeterminate indicators like spinners or skeleton screens. For medium waits (3–10 seconds), use determinate indicators such as progress bars or percentage displays. Consider hybrid approaches for complex loading scenarios. Include clear loading messages (e.g., 'Loading…', 'Please wait') to set user expectations.

subtasks.title: Handle User Interaction During Loading States
subtasks.description: Define and implement strategies for managing user interactions while loading is in progress. This includes blocking or disabling certain UI elements, providing feedback for user actions, and ensuring accessibility.
subtasks.details: Determine when to block the entire UI (e.g., interstitial loading screens for major transitions) versus when to allow partial interaction. Disable or visually dim interactive elements as needed. Ensure loading states are accessible to screen readers and provide appropriate ARIA attributes.

tasks.title: Implement Error Handling for WebContainer Operations
tasks.description: Enhance error handling for file system operations and package installation to ensure robustness and provide clear feedback to users.
tasks.testStrategy:
1. Test error scenarios for each WebContainer operation
2. Verify error messages are user-friendly and informative
3. Test recovery mechanisms for common errors
4. Ensure the application doesn't crash on WebContainer errors
5. Verify error states are properly cleared when operations succeed
6. Test with network interruptions and other edge cases

subtasks.title: Design Error State Management System
subtasks.description: Create a standardized error state management architecture that defines error types, severity levels, and propagation mechanisms
subtasks.details: Develop a consistent structure for error responses including machine-readable error codes, human-readable messages, and correlation IDs. Categorize errors by type (validation, authentication, system, etc.) and severity. Define how errors propagate through the application layers while maintaining appropriate context.

subtasks.title: Implement Operation-Specific Error Handlers
subtasks.description: Create specialized error handling mechanisms for different operations and components in the system
subtasks.details: Analyze each operation to anticipate potential failure points. Implement try/catch blocks with appropriate error recovery logic for each operation type. Create middleware for centralized error handling in API contexts. Ensure security by preventing exposure of sensitive data in error messages.

subtasks.title: Develop User Feedback Mechanisms
subtasks.description: Design and implement systems to communicate errors effectively to end users
subtasks.details: Create descriptive, context-specific error messages that help users understand problems and take corrective actions. Implement different presentation strategies based on error severity and user type. Include links to relevant documentation where appropriate. Ensure feedback is helpful without exposing system internals.

subtasks.title: Establish Error Recovery Strategies
subtasks.description: Implement mechanisms for graceful recovery from different categories of errors
subtasks.details: Design retry mechanisms with exponential backoff for transient failures. Implement circuit breakers to prevent cascading failures. Create fallback mechanisms that provide degraded functionality when primary operations fail. Develop strategies for resource cleanup and state restoration after errors occur.

subtasks.title: Configure Comprehensive Logging and Monitoring
subtasks.description: Set up systems to track, analyze, and learn from errors across the application
subtasks.details: Implement structured logging with appropriate detail levels for different error types. Configure monitoring tools to track error occurrence patterns and frequencies. Set up alerting for critical errors. Establish processes to regularly review error logs to identify recurring issues and improve error handling strategies over time.

tasks.title: Measure and Optimize Performance Metrics
tasks.description: Implement performance measurement and conduct optimization to verify and further improve the loading speed enhancements.
tasks.details:
1. Implement performance measurement for key operations:
```typescript
const startTime = performance.now();
// Operation to measure
const endTime = performance.now();
console.log(`Operation took ${endTime - startTime}ms`);
```
2. Track and log the following metrics:
   - WebContainer boot time
   - File mounting duration
   - Package installation time
   - Total page load to interactive time
3. Create a performance dashboard or logging system
4. Analyze results to identify remaining bottlenecks
5. Implement additional optimizations based on findings
6. Consider implementing user-perceived performance improvements (e.g., skeleton screens)
7. Compare metrics before and after all optimizations
tasks.testStrategy:
1. Establish baseline performance metrics before optimizations
2. Measure performance improvements after each task implementation
3. Test performance across different devices and network conditions
4. Verify user-perceived performance matches actual performance improvements
5. Create automated performance tests for regression testing
6. Document performance improvements with concrete metrics

subtasks.title: Define Clear Performance Objectives and KSIs
subtasks.description: Establish specific, measurable performance objectives and identify the Key Success Indicators (KSIs) that will be used to evaluate performance across all key operations.
subtasks.details: Define both leading and lagging indicators for comprehensive performance measurement. Include metrics for throughput, latency, resource utilization, error rates, and user experience. Ensure objectives are communicated clearly throughout the team to maintain alignment.

subtasks.title: Implement Instrumentation Framework
subtasks.description: Develop and integrate the necessary instrumentation code to capture performance data at critical points in the system.
subtasks.details: Implement low-overhead instrumentation for timing operations, memory usage tracking, thread utilization, I/O operations, and network calls. Ensure instrumentation covers all key operations identified in the objectives phase with minimal impact on the system being measured.

subtasks.title: Develop Data Collection and Storage System
subtasks.description: Create a robust system to collect, validate, and store performance metrics from the instrumentation framework.
subtasks.details: Implement real-time data collection mechanisms, data validation processes, and appropriate storage solutions. Ensure the collection system can handle the volume of metrics without becoming a performance bottleneck itself. Include data retention policies and aggregation strategies.

subtasks.title: Build Analysis Dashboard and Reporting Tools
subtasks.description: Develop visualization and analysis tools to transform raw performance data into actionable insights.
subtasks.details: Create dashboards showing performance trends, anomaly detection, and comparative views. Include filtering capabilities, drill-down analysis, and automated reporting features. Ensure dashboards highlight deviations from the performance measurement baseline.

subtasks.title: Identify and Prioritize Performance Bottlenecks
subtasks.description: Analyze performance data to identify critical bottlenecks and prioritize them based on impact and effort to resolve.
subtasks.details: Conduct systematic analysis of performance data to locate bottlenecks. Categorize issues by severity, impact on user experience, and system resources affected. Create a prioritized optimization plan with estimated impact for each potential improvement.

subtasks.title: Implement Optimizations and Conduct Comparative Testing
subtasks.description: Implement the prioritized optimizations and measure their impact through comparative testing against the baseline.
subtasks.details: Develop and implement optimizations for the identified bottlenecks. Conduct A/B testing to measure improvements against the original baseline. Document optimization techniques used, challenges encountered, and actual performance gains achieved.
